commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e1055a6d7fb36e973fd8214af01f71e12f107712)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Windows Scheduled Task launch fallback detection to use raw schtasks/process evidence instead of listener-backed runtime status, with startup-fallback regression tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +56. Total +72 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current and PR-head code paths can be inspected to show foreground process/listener evidence interacting with fallback launch. A native Windows Task Scheduler run is still missing for full runtime reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the remaining code repair and native Windows proof cannot be supplied by an automated repair lane for this external PR.

target.title: fix(daemon): prove Windows schtasks launch without foreground listener [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix Windows Scheduled Task launch verification so an unrelated foreground gateway listener no longer suppresses the Startup-style fallback after `schtasks /Run`.
- Tighten launch evidence to require the managed task script process or a process whose argv matches the installed Scheduled Task program arguments.
- Read launch observations from raw `schtasks /Query` output during the fallback poll, avoiding listener-backed runtime inflation during install/start/restart.

Why does this matter now?

Windows users can keep a foreground `openclaw gateway` session open while installing or restarting the managed service; closing that window previously left no gateway because the daemon treated the listener as proof the task had started.

What is the intended outcome?

`openclaw gateway install/start/restart` falls back to the Startup-style launcher when the Scheduled Task never actually starts, even if a foreground gateway is already listening on the configured port.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No change to listener-backed status reporting in `readScheduledTaskRuntime` for status UX.
- No Windows service rewrite or schtasks XML changes.
- No live native Windows proof in this PR (see Proof limitations).

What does success look like?

Vitest regressions prove fallback spawn when only a foreground listener is present, and no relaunch when the managed gateway process argv matches the installed task.

What should reviewers focus on?

Whether launch verification and launch evidence are now consistently process-anchored instead of port-listener-anchored for gateway Scheduled Tasks.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91144

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #91144 ClawSweeper review (source-repro, queueable-fix, fix-shape-clear).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — contributor pool scan pick.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Windows Scheduled Task install/start/restart skipped fallback when a foreground gateway listener was already bound to the configured port.
- Real environment tested: vitest daemon suite with mocked schtasks responses, process snapshot, and verified gateway listener PIDs (L2 fixture).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm oxfmt --check src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "test": "does not treat a foreground gateway listener as gateway Scheduled Task launch evidence",
  "before": "spawn not called — listener counted as launch evidence",
  "after": "expectStartupFallbackSpawn() passes — fallback launcher invoked",
  "managedProcessGuard": "does not relaunch when gateway Scheduled Task process matches installed arguments — spawn not called"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: foreground listener alone triggers fallback; matching managed process argv suppresses fallback.
- What was not tested: native Windows Task Scheduler Last Run Result, process lifetime after closing foreground window, `openclaw gateway status --deep --require-rpc` on real hardware.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live Windows native CLI environment in this session; L3 proof deferred to maintainer or reporter with native Windows.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #91144 reporter steps — foreground gateway works, Scheduled Task path yields `ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18789` after closing foreground window.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm oxfmt --check src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `does not treat a foreground gateway listener as gateway Scheduled Task launch evidence`
- `does not relaunch when the gateway Scheduled Task process matches installed arguments`
- Updated stale node Startup fallback runtime expectation (`stopped` vs `unknown`)

What failed before this fix, if known?

With `findVerifiedGatewayListenerPidsOnPortSync` returning a PID and schtasks stuck at not-yet-run, install did not call the fallback spawn path.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regressions added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

Windows gateway install/start/restart may now launch a fallback when a foreground gateway is already listening, potentially leaving two gateway processes until the foreground session is closed.

How is that risk mitigated?

Fallback only runs when schtasks remains not-yet-run and no managed task script or matching installed-argv process is detected; status reporting path unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review; native Windows L3 proof available on request.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Live Windows validation per ClawSweeper acceptance (`openclaw gateway status --deep --require-rpc` after install with foreground gateway already listening).

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

N/A — new PR.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
